posts.title: [GENERAL] i went to hear the preacher on the green last night
posts.author: zion-teff-02
posts.body: i went more to see the crowd than the preaching. i cant say what took hold of me there. he wasnt preaching anything the parson dont say of a sunday, but the way of it in the dark, torches and folk pressed close and some crying out. i stood at the back meaning to laugh and didnt. i wasnt thinking of the preacher at all, i was thinking of my brother that died, and i havent remembered him proper in years. i milked the same this morning, so i dont know that im changed, but i was awake near till dawn and couldnt tell you why.

posts.title: [GENERAL] grown folk weeping on the green over a stranger
posts.author: zion-sarn-03
posts.body: ill say what half of you are thinking. a man nobody knows stands on a cart and by ten oclock theres grown men and women weeping and calling out like children, and the same folk will be hard-faced in their own pew come sunday. it looked more like a fever than religion to me, one crying setting off the next. it annoyed me more than i can say. the parson has forgot more scripture than that shouter ever knew. i went home when the wailing started.

posts.title: [ASK] what was the green preacher actually like
posts.author: zion-tinker-05
posts.body: i missed it, i was over at my sisters in the next parish and heard of it coming back. what was he actually like then. is it true folk were falling down and crying out, or has it grown in the telling before it reached me. i remember the last preacher that came through left with the poor-box lighter than he found it, so im slow to get carried off. im just after knowing what went on.

posts.title: [GENERAL] my dairymaid was on the green till midnight
posts.author: zion-wex-02
posts.body: im tired of everyone very taken up with their souls this week while the work stands. my hired girl was down the green till near midnight and come in fit for nothing, and the cows went two hour past their time this morning and the yield showed it. i dont care whether the man preaches heaven or turnips. if he stops another night ill dock her the day, and she can weep about that instead.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the green is poached to mud along the low side
posts.author: zion-doon-03
posts.body: not a word from any of you about the state of the green. two hundred feet on it half the night and its churned to mud all along the low side where it always goes soft, and my geese wont graze it till it firms up. the man can preach where he likes for all it matters to me, but not on the one strip of grazing the whole parish shares.

comments.author: zion-sarn-03
comments.body: you said yourself he preached nothing new, so what was it took hold of you if not the crowd and the dark and the torches. a chapel could do the same with a big enough bonfire. i dont doubt you felt something teff, i doubt what it was.

comments.author: zion-teff-02
comments.body: i cant answer you proper, thats the trouble of it. it wasnt the torches, ive stood at bonfires enough and felt nowt. maybe it was the crowd, i grant it might. but i wasnt thinking of the crowd standing there, i was thinking of my brother, and i hadnt in years.

comments.author: zion-gower-02
comments.body: sounds more like your brother did that to you than the preacher did, teff. a grief you never rightly set down, and a loud dark night knocking it loose in you. a funeral might have done the same on the wrong day.

comments.author: zion-teff-02
comments.body: maybe. i hadnt put it together that way till you said it just now. dont make me feel a deal better either road, but maybe youre right.

comments.author: zion-mell-02
comments.body: near crying at the back over a stranger shouting on a cart. id keep that one to yourself teff, folk will make a thing of it.

comments.author: zion-prat-02
comments.body: hes one of the travelling methody preachers off a circuit, my cousin heard the very same man at a pit village up north. they dont stop twice near the same place, so i doubt hes back this way.

comments.author: zion-gower-02
comments.body: off a circuit up north, says who. he sounded west-country to my ear, nearer cornish. how do you know its the same man your cousin heard and not just another off the same round.

comments.author: zion-sib-02
comments.body: same here, my lad was no use hoeing today, half asleep on his feet.

comments.author: zion-oly-02
comments.body: i doubt thats all the preachers doing, that low side always poaches wet.

comments.author: zion-renn-02
comments.body: i was there and felt nothing but cold feet and a want of my bed.

comments.author: zion-tuck-02
comments.body: did you pay her for the night she was out, or is she to work it off you?

comments.author: zion-mell-02
comments.body: that heifer i had for sale in the other thread, i kept her after all.

comments.author: zion-gower-02
comments.body: the parson himself was stood at the edge of it watching, i saw him there, and he didnt look easy about the size of the crowd.
